_note: Status 'mapped' means the component has been mapped to this criterion internally. It does not mean the criterion has been verified by a formal audit. Run audit-rules.json checks and update validation_status before changing overall_status.

criteria.title: Non-text Content
criteria.evidence: Divider is a visual line with no non-text content (images, icons). When decorative, aria-hidden='true' suppresses AT announcement. When semantic, <hr> provides role='separator' with no name required.

criteria.title: Info and Relationships
criteria.evidence: <hr> provides implicit role='separator'. Programmatic relationship between divided sections is established by document structure. Vertical dividers use aria-orientation='vertical'.

criteria.title: Use of Color
criteria.evidence: RISK: Color variants (neutral, primary, alpha-white, white) must not be used as the sole means of conveying information. Contract documents this as consumer responsibility. Component itself does not convey information through color.

criteria.title: Non-text Contrast
criteria.evidence: Neutral divider (#D2D6DB on white) = ~1.4:1. This is below the 3:1 minimum for UI components under WCAG 1.4.11. However, the divider is decorative (not a UI component required for interaction). Primary divider (#1B8354 on white) = ~5.4:1 — passes. Formal audit required for specific usage contexts.

criteria.title: Keyboard
criteria.evidence: Divider is not interactive and does not receive keyboard focus. No keyboard operability requirements apply.

criteria.title: Name, Role, Value
criteria.evidence: Semantic: <hr> provides role='separator' implicitly. Name not required for separators. Vertical: aria-orientation='vertical' communicates direction. Decorative: role='none' aria-hidden='true' removes from AT tree.

standards.name: Platform Code Internal Design System Standards

criteria.title: Semantic element usage
criteria.evidence: template.html uses <hr class='divider'>. Vertical uses aria-orientation='vertical'. Audit rule PLATFORM-CODE-DIVIDER-001 enforces this.

criteria.title: Decorative dividers aria-hidden
criteria.evidence: Contract documents role='none' aria-hidden='true' for decorative use. Consumer responsibility.

criteria.title: Component CSS uses component-level semantic tokens only
criteria.evidence: divider.css uses --divider-* tokens throughout with inline fallbacks. All 5 tokens documented as missing from token.css in analysis.md.

variant_compliance_notes.note: Neutral color (#D2D6DB on white) = ~1.4:1. Acceptable for decorative separators. Not suitable for focus indicators or informational use.
Designed for dark/colored backgrounds. Contrast on dark bg must be verified in deployment context.
Designed for dark/colored backgrounds. Pure white (#FFF) on dark backgrounds will meet contrast requirements. Verify in deployment context.
All vertical variants require aria-orientation='vertical' when semantic. Consumer must apply this attribute.
